page: Microsoft Azure E2_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Ev5 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to a single physical core with 2 threads per core, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but limited multi-core scaling due to the low core count. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the average category, supported by hardware features like AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. However, multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench is poor, as are memory bandwidth tests, which register 14.91 GB/sec for large block reads and 13.55 GB/sec for large block writes. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also in the lower tiers, with Redis achieving 1.98 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E2_v5 offers a high memory capacity relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require heavy parallel processing. Given the lack of local storage and the poor multi-core performance, this instance is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, lightweight web serving, or memory-intensive background tasks that run efficiently on a dual-core configuration.
description: Microsoft Azure E2_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev5 family, featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show average single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench, but poor multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. It is equipped with advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AES cryptography acceleration. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads with low parallel processing demands, making it suitable for small databases, caching layers, and lightweight web serving.

tagline: A memory-optimized dual-core virtual machine offering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight, memory-bound enterprise workloads.
